# throwback / CrudApp — AI Agent Reference

> Dense reference for AI coding agents. See README.md for human-readable docs.

## What this framework provides

- Perl CGI JSON API framework for shared hosting (no Node, no Docker required)
- Python/FastAPI alternative backend — same DB schema, same wire protocol, interchangeable
- Per-user access control via `access_rules` JSON stored in `_auth`
- Automatic CRUD endpoints from a single method call (`shift->crud('tablename')`)
- Login rate limiting, email verification, SMTP email, API proxy to upstream services
- CLI (`throwback`) to scaffold, initdb, and run a local dev server
- Shared JS client (`crud-client.js`) works against both backends unchanged

## What AI should NOT do

- Do not put secrets in `access_rules` JSON — it is stored in the DB, visible to the user
- Do not generate `crudapp.conf` with real credentials — use environment variable overrides in production
- Do not skip the `.throwback` bootstrap for Python apps — importing without it causes `ModuleNotFoundError`
- Do not generate `schema.sql` with only `_auth` and `_sessions` — `_login_attempts` and `_email_verifications` are also required system tables
- Do not use `throwback initdb` for MySQL production — it is SQLite-only; use `schema.sql` directly
- Do not assume port 3000 for the Python backend — Perl is 3000, Python is 3001

## Architecture

```
Browser (JS crud-client.js)
    │
    ├── :3000  →  Perl backend (throwback run / Apache CGI)
    └── :3001  →  Python backend (uvicorn)
                    Both backends share the same SQLite/MySQL database
```

The `.throwback` file in every project directory contains the absolute path to the framework repo. Both Perl and Python use it to locate the framework without installing a package.

## System Tables (all required)

All backends require these four tables. Generated by `throwback create` / `throwback initdb`.

```sql
-- Users
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _auth (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,   -- INT AUTO_INCREMENT for MySQL
    username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_salt TEXT NOT NULL,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,            -- SHA256(salt + password)
    access_rules TEXT,                      -- JSON; see Access Rules below
    email TEXT,
    email_verified INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Sessions
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _sessions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,             -- SHA256(32 random bytes)
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL,           -- 7 days from login
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES _auth(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Login rate limiting (required by auth.py; used by Perl too)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _login_attempts (
    username TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    failed_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    locked_until INTEGER   -- Unix timestamp (int); NULL = not locked
);

-- Email verification tokens
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _email_verifications (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    token TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES _auth(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
```

## CLI

```bash
throwback create <dir>   # scaffold project, git init, copy llms.txt
throwback initdb         # create SQLite DB from schema_sqlite.sql; add demo user
throwback run            # start Perl dev server on :3000
throwback deploy         # print production deployment steps
```

Generated project layout:
```
myapp/
├── MyApp.pm             # API module (subclass CrudApp)
├── api.cgi              # CGI entry point (production)
├── .htaccess            # Apache routing + Authorization header passthrough
├── index.html           # Starter frontend
├── js/crud-client.js    # JS API client
├── schema.sql           # MySQL schema (production)
├── schema_sqlite.sql    # SQLite schema (local dev / throwback initdb)
├── crudapp.conf         # Dev server config
├── .throwback           # Absolute path to throwback framework repo
└── .gitignore
```

## Configuration (crudapp.conf)

```ini
[database]
type = sqlite
path = ./dev.db

[server]
port = 3000        ; Perl backend (throwback run)
; port = 3001      ; Python backend (uv run uvicorn main:app --reload)
static_dir = .
app_url = http://localhost:3000   ; used to build email verification links
app_name = My App

[smtp]
host = smtp.example.com
port = 587
user = noreply@example.com
pass = secret
from = noreply@example.com
from_name = My App
starttls = 1
```

Environment variables override all INI values:
`CRUDAPP_DB_TYPE`, `CRUDAPP_DB_PATH`, `CRUDAPP_PORT`, `CRUDAPP_APP_URL`,
`CRUDAPP_SMTP_HOST`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_PORT`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_USER`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_PASS`,
`CRUDAPP_SMTP_FROM`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_FROM_NAME`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_SSL`, `CRUDAPP_SMTP_STARTTLS`

---

## Perl Backend

### API module pattern

```perl
package MyApp;
use strict;
use warnings;
use base 'CrudApp';

sub configure {
    my $app = shift;
    $app->set_db('yourdb', 'user', 'password');  # MySQL (production only)
    # SQLite is configured automatically from crudapp.conf in dev
}

# Expose CRUD endpoints — one line per table
sub todos { shift->crud('todos') }
sub posts { shift->crud('posts') }

# Optional: anonymous (unauthenticated) read access
sub anon_access {
    return { posts => ['read'] };
}

# Custom endpoint
sub dashboard {
    my $app  = shift;
    my $user = $app->current_user or return $app->render_error("Unauthorized", 401);
    $app->render_json({
        total     => $app->query_value("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM todos WHERE user_id = ?", $user->{id}),
        completed => $app->query_value("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM todos WHERE user_id = ? AND done = 1", $user->{id}),
    });
}

# Hook: set to 1 to require email_verified before login (default: 0)
sub require_email_verified { 1 }

1;
```

### Perl helper methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `$app->current_user` | Returns user row hashref or undef |
| `$app->crud('table')` | Handles GET/POST/DELETE for a table with access_rules enforcement |
| `$app->render_json($data)` | Output JSON response |
| `$app->render_error($msg, $status)` | Output JSON error |
| `$app->param('key')` | Read query string or POST body param |
| `$app->query_row($sql, @bind)` | Fetch one row as hashref |
| `$app->query_rows($sql, @bind)` | Fetch all rows as arrayref of hashrefs |
| `$app->query_value($sql, @bind)` | Fetch single scalar |
| `$app->query_do($sql, @bind)` | Execute non-SELECT (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) |
| `$app->create_user($u, $pw, $rules)` | Hash+salt password, insert into `_auth` |
| `$app->create_email_verification($uid)` | Create token in `_email_verifications`, return token |
| `$app->consume_email_verification($token)` | Verify token, set `email_verified=1`, return user_id or undef |
| `$app->require_email_verified` | Override returning 1 to gate login on verified email |
| `$app->_sql_now()` | `datetime('now')` (SQLite) / `NOW()` (MySQL) |
| `$app->_sql_from_unixtime()` | `datetime(?, 'unixepoch')` / `FROM_UNIXTIME(?)` |

### Built-in Perl endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /login | No | `{username, password}` → `{token, expires}` |
| POST | /logout | Yes | Invalidate current token |
| POST | /register | No | `{username, password, email}` → starts email verification if `require_email_verified` is set |
| GET | /verify_email/:token | No | Consume verification token, set `email_verified=1` |

### API proxy (Perl)

Gate upstream API calls (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) behind CrudApp auth:

```perl
__PACKAGE__->add_proxy('claude', 'https://api.anthropic.com', $ENV{ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}, {
    headers => { 'x-api-key' => '$KEY', 'anthropic-version' => '2023-06-01' }
});
sub claude { shift->proxy('claude') }
# Client: api.request('POST', 'claude/v1/messages', {...})
```

---

## Python Backend

The `python/` directory is a FastAPI peer to the Perl backend. It is **interchangeable**: a session token minted by Perl is valid on Python and vice versa (identical hashing algorithm).

### Bootstrap (how a project imports the Python modules)

```python
from pathlib import Path

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent          # your project directory
throwback_path = (BASE_DIR / ".throwback").read_text().strip()

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(throwback_path) / "python"))

from config import load_config
from database import make_engine, get_db_dependency, get_row, get_rows, insert_row, update_row, delete_row
from auth import make_get_current_user, check_rate_limit, record_login_failure, clear_login_failures, hash_password, generate_salt, generate_token
from email_utils import send_template_email
```

### Starting the Python backend

```bash
cd myapp/python
uv run uvicorn main:app --reload --port 3001
# or: uvicorn main:app --reload --port 3001
```

Port convention: Perl = **3000**, Python = **3001**. Both serve the same frontend (the shared `example/` or your project's static files). Visit `:3000` → Perl, `:3001` → Python.

### Python module reference

**`config.py`**
```python
load_config(config_file: str | None = None) -> AppConfig
# Reads crudapp.conf from repo root, then CRUDAPP_* env vars override.
# AppConfig.database / .server / .smtp are dataclasses with the same fields
# as the INI sections.
```

**`database.py`**
```python
make_engine(cfg: AppConfig, base_dir: Path | None = None) -> Engine
# IMPORTANT: base_dir controls how a relative database.path is resolved.
# Pass your project's BASE_DIR when importing from an external app, e.g.:
#   engine = make_engine(config, base_dir=BASE_DIR)
# If omitted it defaults to the throwback repo root — only correct for the
# reference main.py. Alternatively, set an absolute path via CRUDAPP_DB_PATH.

get_db_dependency(engine: Engine) -> callable   # Returns FastAPI Depends generator
get_row(conn, table, where: dict) -> dict | None
get_rows(conn, table, where: dict, limit=20, offset=0) -> list[dict]
insert_row(conn, table, data: dict) -> int      # returns lastrowid
update_row(conn, table, row_id: int, data: dict) -> int   # returns rowcount
delete_row(conn, table, where: dict) -> int     # returns rowcount
```

**`auth.py`**
```python
hash_password(salt: str, password: str) -> str  # SHA256(salt+password)
generate_token() -> str                         # SHA256(32 random bytes)
generate_salt() -> str                          # first 32 hex chars of token
extract_bearer_token(authorization: str | None) -> str | None
make_get_current_user(get_db) -> callable       # FastAPI dependency factory; raises 401
check_rate_limit(conn, username: str) -> str | None   # None = ok; str = error msg
record_login_failure(conn, username: str) -> None     # exponential backoff
clear_login_failures(conn, username: str) -> None     # on successful login
```

**`email_utils.py`**
```python
render_template(template_path, vars: dict) -> tuple[str, str]  # (subject, body)
# Template format: first line "Subject: ...", blank line, then body.
# {{variable}} placeholders; missing vars become "".

send_email(cfg: SmtpConfig, to: str, subject: str, body: str) -> None
# Supports SSL (cfg.ssl=True, port 465) and STARTTLS (cfg.starttls=True, port 587).
# Raises on failure — callers should catch and log.

send_template_email(cfg, to, template_path, vars: dict) -> None
```

### Register flow: Python vs Perl

The Python reference `main.py` requires `email` in the register body and gates login on `email_verified = 1`. The Perl `CrudApp` has a `require_email_verified()` hook that defaults to **0** (off).

| Aspect | Perl | Python reference |
|--------|------|-----------------|
| Register requires email | No (optional) | Yes |
| Email verification on login | Off by default; set `require_email_verified` to 1 | Always on |
| `create_user()` helper | Yes (`$app->create_user($u, $pw, $rules)`) | No — write the INSERT manually |
| Register response | `{token, expires}` (logged in immediately if no verification) | `{ok, message}` — must verify email first |

For a Python app that does **not** want email verification, omit the `email_verified` check in the login handler and accept any user after correct password.

### Access rules and filters in Python

`access_rules` JSON filter enforcement (`"filters": {"user_id": "$user.id"}`) is **automatic** in the Perl `crud()` method. In Python, there is no equivalent auto-enforcement — custom endpoints must apply filters manually. The reference `main.py` provides a helper:

```python
def get_user_filters(user: dict) -> dict:
    """Resolve access_rules JSON filters for the current user."""
    rules = json.loads(user.get("access_rules") or "{}")
    raw = rules.get("tables", {}).get("todos", {}).get("filters", {})
    resolved = {}
    for k, v in raw.items():
        if v == "$user.id":
            resolved[k] = user["id"]
        elif isinstance(v, str) and v.startswith("$user."):
            resolved[k] = user.get(v[len("$user."):])
        else:
            resolved[k] = v
    return resolved

# Use it:
filters = get_user_filters(user)
rows = get_rows(db, "todos", filters, limit, offset)
```

For multi-table apps, generalize `get_user_filters` to accept a table name argument.

---

## Access Rules JSON

Stored in `_auth.access_rules`. Controls what the Perl `crud()` method allows and what the Python backend uses for filter resolution.

```json
{
  "tables": {
    "todos": {
      "access": ["create", "read", "update", "delete"],
      "filters": {"user_id": "$user.id"}
    },
    "posts": {
      "access": ["read"]
    }
  }
}
```

- `"$user.id"` is replaced with the authenticated user's `id` at runtime
- `"$user.X"` resolves any field X from the user row
- Tables prefixed `_` are always blocked by Perl's `crud()` regardless of access rules
- `anon_access()` on the Perl module allows unauthenticated reads for listed tables

---

## CRUD Endpoint Behaviour (both backends)

| Method | Path | Auth | Behaviour |
|--------|------|------|-----------|
| POST | /login | No | `{username, password}` → `{token, expires}` |
| POST | /logout | Yes | Delete session token |
| GET | /table | Yes | `{data: [...], limit, offset}` — filters from access_rules applied |
| GET | /table/:id | Yes | Single row or 404 |
| POST | /table | Yes | Body with id → update (200); body without id → create (201) |
| DELETE | /table/:id | Yes | `{deleted: id}` or 404 |

---

## SQL Portability (Perl helpers)

Use these in Perl instead of hardcoding SQLite/MySQL syntax:

```perl
$app->_sql_now()             # datetime('now')    vs  NOW()
$app->_sql_from_unixtime()   # datetime(?, 'unixepoch')  vs  FROM_UNIXTIME(?)
```

---

## File Structure

```
throwback/
├── bin/throwback            # CLI
├── CrudApp.pm               # Perl framework core
├── CrudApp/
│   ├── Config.pm            # INI config loader
│   ├── DB.pm                # DB adapter factory
│   ├── DevServer.pm         # Local HTTP server
│   ├── Email.pm             # SMTP mailer
│   └── DB/
│       ├── MySQL.pm
│       └── SQLite.pm
├── python/                  # Python FastAPI peer backend
│   ├── main.py              # Reference app (todo example, port 3001)
│   ├── auth.py              # Password, token, rate limiting
│   ├── database.py          # SQLAlchemy engine + raw SQL helpers
│   ├── config.py            # INI config loader (mirrors CrudApp::Config)
│   └── email_utils.py       # Template email (mirrors CrudApp::Email)
├── example/                 # Shared example todo app
│   ├── MyApi.pm
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── js/crud-client.js
│   └── email_templates/verify_email.txt
├── dev/
│   ├── schema_sqlite.sql    # Full schema for the example app
│   ├── setup.pl             # Setup script for the example app
│   └── runner.pl            # Dev server runner for the example app
└── llms.txt                 # This file
```
